requirements.title: Named governed acceptance policy
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying-party decision requires a named, version-controlled governed acceptance policy with defined decision authority and stable scope.
requirements.reason: The v0.2.8 acceptance policy is a fictional local demo policy authored for ProofRail. No external governed acceptance policy is established by the v0.3.0 handoff.

requirements.title: Named acceptance authority
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying-party decision requires a named acceptance authority with declared scope, accountability, and a documented authority binding.
requirements.reason: The v0.2.8 fixture relying party (demo.relying_party.local_reviewer) is fictional. No real acceptance authority is bound by the Silver chain.

requirements.title: Independent verifier identity binding
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying party requires a named, key-bound, independent verifier identity with rotation discipline and an accountable operator.
requirements.reason: Silver v0.2.2 verifier output attestation provides a hash-anchored identity binding from a demo attestor key to verification outputs. The attestor key is a local demo key, not a governed verifier identity.

requirements.title: Evidence retention policy
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold acceptance requires a documented evidence retention policy specifying duration, format, storage discipline, and hand-off rules.
requirements.reason: The v0.3.0 handoff is a portable package. It does not establish a retention policy or storage discipline.

requirements.title: Change control policy over fixtures and verifier code
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying-party decision requires documented change control over authority fixtures, harness scripts, packager configuration, and verifier code that produced the accepted evidence.
requirements.reason: The Silver chain is reproducible from the repository, but no change-control attestation accompanies the v0.3.0 handoff package.

requirements.title: Operational revocation handling
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying-party decision requires operational revocation handling: real revocation infrastructure, propagation, and acknowledged operator procedures.
requirements.reason: Silver v0.2.9 provides a local revocation/challenge drill bound to a v0.2.8 acceptance package. The drill is a local evidence artifact, not an operational revocation process.

requirements.title: Challenge and dispute resolution process
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying-party decision requires a defined challenge / dispute resolution process with named adjudicators and recorded outcomes.
requirements.reason: Silver v0.2.9 classifies within-window challenges against the acceptance record's challenge window and records review triggers, but does not adjudicate any challenge.

requirements.title: Audit trail and independent review
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold acceptance requires a tamper-evident audit trail and an independent review process operated by an accountable party.
requirements.reason: Silver v0.2.4 and v0.2.5 produce hash-anchored harness and trust-boundary evidence. There is no independent review process operated by an accountable party.

requirements.title: Runtime operating boundary evidence
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold relying-party decision requires evidence that the runtime substrate enforced the same authority semantics observed in the Silver demo.
requirements.reason: Silver v0.2.4 / v0.2.7 produce evidence from a deterministic harness and a simulated gateway adapter. No real runtime substrate is exercised.

requirements.title: External accountability binding
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold acceptance requires a binding to an externally accountable operator with stated obligations and an external recourse path.
requirements.reason: The v0.3.0 handoff is bound to a fictional local relying party. No external accountability is established.

requirements.title: Public or shared acceptance record
requirements.gold_prerequisite: A Gold acceptance requires a public or otherwise shared acceptance / rejection record with stable references.
requirements.reason: The Silver chain produces local hash-anchored records only. No public or shared acceptance ledger is established.

requirements.title: Legal or contractual basis for reliance
requirements.gold_prerequisite: Legal effect or contractual basis for reliance is established outside ProofRail by the relying party and its counterparties.
requirements.reason: ProofRail does not assert legal effect, contractual reliance, or transferred trust. Legal basis is established by external parties.

requirements.title: Production use authorization
requirements.gold_prerequisite: Production use authorization is granted outside ProofRail by an accountable operator under its own change-management process.
requirements.reason: ProofRail does not authorize production use. Production authorization is granted by external parties under their own process.

scope_limitations: This requirement set is a local ProofRail demo boundary inventory, not an external compliance standard.
Domain coverage is fixed at thirteen domains for the v0.3.1 demo; it does not enumerate every governance prerequisite a real relying party would adopt.
Silver evidence references are pointers to ProofRail Silver artifact types, not assertions of operational use.
non_claims: This requirement set is not a Gold certification standard.
This requirement set is not a Gold readiness scorecard.
This requirement set is not legal advice, regulator approval, or auditor approval.
This requirement set is not a compliance crosswalk to any external framework.
silver_evidence_present means relevant Silver evidence is present, not that the Gold prerequisite is satisfied.
